prompt: What are the pathophysiological mechanisms underlying Marfan syndrome? I want to understand the genes involved, the molecular mechanisms, key clinical phenotypes, and any therapeutic targets tracked in DisMech.

assertions.text: Mentions FBN1 or fibrillin-1 as the causative gene
assertions.description: The response correctly identifies FBN1 (fibrillin-1) as the primary gene involved in Marfan syndrome

assertions.text: Describes connective tissue or extracellular matrix as the affected biological system
assertions.description: The response identifies connective tissue and/or ECM as the primary affected system

assertions.text: Lists at least 3 distinct clinical phenotypes (e.g., aortic aneurysm, ectopia lentis, skeletal features, MVP, tall stature, scoliosis, dural ectasia)
assertions.description: The response enumerates at least 3 specific clinical phenotypes of Marfan syndrome

assertions.text: Mentions at least one therapeutic target or treatment approach (e.g., losartan, TGF-beta pathway, beta-blockers, surgical intervention)
assertions.description: The response identifies at least one therapeutic target or treatment strategy

assertions.text: With-skill response is substantially more specific and grounded in DisMech data than without-skill baseline
assertions.description: The with-skill response cites actual DisMech data (genes, HPO terms, evidence PMIDs) rather than giving a generic textbook answer
